title: Schoberia pallasiana

This family consists of around 83 genera and more than 800 species. Schoberia pallasiana is native to tropical Africa and is part of the genus Schoberia, which consists of only two species. The classification of Schoberia pallasiana is also supported by its morphological characteristics, such as its fruit and leaf structure. intro: Schoberia pallasiana, also known as the Pallas Schoberia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Anacardiaceae, native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may help alleviate symptoms of various diseases such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Schoberia pallasiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
